title: Calls to methods should not trigger an exception
description: This rule checks that method calls are not made in a way that can trigger exceptions. Unexpected exceptions from method calls can lead to program instability and unpredictable behavior. Methods should be called with proper arguments and in valid states to avoid exceptions.
recommendation: Review all method calls to ensure that they cannot throw unexpected exceptions. Validate input parameters and object states before invoking methods.
impact: If a method call triggers an exception, it may cause the application to crash or behave incorrectly, leading to reliability and stability issues.

examples.bad: String s = null; int len = s.length(); // NullPointerException
examples.good: String s = getString(); if (s != null) { int len = s.length(); }

checks.message: Review all method calls to ensure that they cannot throw unexpected exceptions. Validate input parameters and object states before invoking methods.
